content.problemStatement:
Janmansh has to submit 333 assignments for Chingari before 101010 pm and he starts to do the assignments at XXX pm. Each assignment takes him 111 hour to complete. Can you tell whether he'll be able to complete all assignments on time or not?


Input Format

The first line will contain TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains one integer XXX - the time when Janmansh starts doing the assignments.


Output Format
For each test case, output `Yes` if he can complete the assignments on time. Otherwise, output `No`.


You may print each character of `Yes` and `No` in uppercase or lowercase (for example, `yes`, `yEs`, `YES` will be considered identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer X (1 ≤ X ≤ 9) representing the time (in pm) when Janmansh starts working.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output "Yes" if Janmansh can finish all assignments before 10 pm, otherwise output "No". Each answer should be on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: Starting at 7 pm, Janmansh finishes at 7+3=10 pm, exactly the deadline → Yes.\nTest case 2: Starting at 9 pm, finish time is 12 pm > 10 pm → No.
Even though 10 is outside the given constraints, the algorithm correctly reports No because 10+3=13 >10.

solution.approach: Each assignment takes exactly 1 hour and there are 3 assignments. Janmansh finishes at time X + 3. He meets the deadline if X + 3 ≤ 10. For every test case we just check this inequality and print the appropriate answer.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
